intro: Monvillea euchlora, also known as the "Venus Flytrap of the Andes," is a rare and unique succulent plant native to the Andes mountains in South America. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Monvillea euchlora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
